bullets.text: Annual CR 93 — 3 pts better than 96 target, aided by favorable prior-year development; net income $1B+ ($1.59/share); Gainshare score 1.1; variable dividend ~$250M ($0.41/share) paid to shareholders.

NPW surpassed $15B for first time in company history; 5% annual growth (~$670M); Q4 growth 8% — strongest quarterly result in 26 quarters; Commercial Auto finished +6% for the year after double-digit H2 growth.

Snapshot launched nationally — 'statistics of one' personalized pricing via driving behavior data; miles of observation equivalent to 'multiple round trips to Mars'; patents approved; accepted by regulators in 39 states + D.C.

PIFs grew ~580,000 for the year (~1M new customers); agency-produced auto reached all-time high PIF, correcting negative trend from 2006; Direct PIF grew 6%; special lines exceeded $1.2B written premiums.

$500M 10-year senior notes issued at 3.75% coupon; 51.3M shares repurchased (~$1B, ~7.7% of outstanding); debt-to-total capital 29.6%; $350M debt tranche retired early 2012; investment total return 3.2% FTE.

Flo voted America's #1 advertising icon by Entertainment Weekly; 3.3M+ Facebook fans; 'Messenger' campaign reworked mid-year for real-world unscripted settings; new commercials inventory built for 2011-2012 use.

Mobile strategy 'mobilize not miniaturize' — camera-capture prototype for DL and insurance card enables instant quotes; Name Your Price plus Snapshot creates compelling differentiated quote experience unavailable from competitors.

Personal Lines 55% agency / 45% direct — uniquely balanced single-brand multi-distribution model; agent acceptance of Snapshot growing; Name Your Price available across both channels with consistent brand presentation.

Claims organization handled multiple 2011 catastrophes with remarkable logistics; catastrophe customers showed higher renewal rates than control group — measurable NPS and retention uplift from excellent disaster response.

Industry CR estimated at 100+ for 2011 with minimal premium growth (~1.5%); Progressive positioned to outperform as market hardens; 75th anniversary year described as 'beginning' of new chapter in product and technology evolution.
